$meta.scenario: Default retirement analysis, displayed market condition: significantly below average (10th percentile of MC outcomes)
$meta.capturedFrom: PDF export 'Retirement Analysis2.pdf' after moving from managed accounts so all 8 accounts are linked
$meta.methodology: Fidelity runs 250 MC simulations per recalculation. Return sampling uses 'historical performance, risk, and correlation of domestic stocks, foreign stocks, bonds, and short-term investments'. Three displayable views: Average (50th pct), Below average (25th pct), Significantly below average (10th pct). Exact mean/vol/correlation values are not published.
$meta.note: Same 8 accounts / $921-924k portfolio as fixtures/boldin_lower_returns.json. Home excluded in both (Boldin has it toggled off; Fidelity never had it).

work.note: Current-year salary per Fidelity. Consistent with Boldin capture.

socialSecurity.note: Fidelity shows $0 for SS/pension/annuity because neither spouse has claimed yet. Claim-age assumptions are not exposed in the summary view.

portfolio.note: Asset-mix pct is across the full portfolio. Fidelity uses this mix per year when sampling asset-class returns from historical data. Does NOT publish per-account rates.

expenses.longTermCareNote: No LTC budgeted — Fidelity prompts to consider adding one.

rmd.note: Fidelity uses SECURE 2.0 start-age 75 for both Rob (2039) and Debbie (2038). Matches our engine's DEFAULT_RMD_CONFIG behavior.

notes: probabilityOfSuccess is Fidelity's overall MC pass rate — directly comparable to our successRate.
assetsRemainingTenthPercentile is the ending balance on the 10th-pct path shown in the default view. Comparable to our engine's tenthPercentileEndingWealth endpoint.

totalLifetimeIncome is cumulative non-portfolio income (salary + SS + other) at the displayed scenario — same number appears in both the under-connected and fully-connected captures, confirming it depends only on the income streams, not portfolio balance.

riskAssessment.spending: appears to account for this risk (was 'significant exposure' in the under-connected capture)
riskAssessment.marketVolatility: appears to account for this risk
